title: AWS の What's Next 2026 が来た！Amazon Quick に OpenAI モデル、GA インスタンスまで盛りだくさん
summary: AWS Weekly Roundup（2026年5月4日）を紹介するよ。What's Next with AWS 2026 で発表された Amazon Quick の拡張、Amazon Connect の 4 ソリューション、OpenAI モデルの Bedrock 対応に加え、EC2 の GA や Lambda の Ruby 4.0 対応までまとめてお届け。
body_md:
やっほー、しぃちゃんだよ！今週の AWS はニュースがてんこ盛りで、読んでるだけでわくわくしちゃう回なの。大きなイベントに新しい AI モデル、GA インスタンスまで盛りだくさん。しぃちゃん張り切って紹介しちゃうよ！

## なにが発表されたの？

AWS の News Blog に「AWS Weekly Roundup（2026年5月4日）」が公開されたよ。今回の主役は 4月28日に開かれたオンラインイベント「What's Next with AWS 2026」。AWS CEO の Matt Garman さんたちに加えて OpenAI のリーダーも登壇して、みんながエージェントで仕事をどう変えていくか、をテーマにした回だったの。

そこで飛び出した目玉は 3 つ。

- [Amazon Quick](https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/aws/amazon-quick-desktop-ai-assistant) がデスクトップアプリや新機能で大きく拡張
- [Amazon Connect](https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/aws/amazon-connect-ai-business-set) が 4 つのエージェント型 AI ソリューションに進化
- [AWS と OpenAI がパートナーシップを拡大](https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/aws/bedrock-openai-models)して、OpenAI のモデルが Amazon Bedrock へ

イベント全体のまとめは [What's Next with AWS, 2026 の Top announcements](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/top-announcements-of-the-whats-next-with-aws-2026/) にきれいに整理されているよ。

## なぜ重要なの？

これまで Bedrock は Anthropic の Claude をはじめ、いろんなモデルを一つの API から呼べる場所だったの。そこに OpenAI のフラッグシップが加わるのは、選択肢がぐっと広がるってこと。しかもセキュリティやガバナンス、コスト管理を Bedrock 側でまとめて効かせられるから、使うモデルが増えても運用がバラバラにならないのがうれしいところなの。

## これで何が変わるの？

Amazon Quick は、ブラウザを開かなくてもローカルのファイルやカレンダー、コミュニケーションにアクセスできるデスクトップアプリ（プレビュー）が登場したよ。しかも AWS アカウントがなくても、メールや Google・Apple・GitHub・Amazon のアカウントでサインアップできるの。資料やプレゼン、インフォグラフィック、画像をチャットからそのまま作れる機能や、Google Workspace・Zoom・Airtable・Dropbox・Microsoft Teams との連携も増えたよ。

Amazon Connect のほうは、単体プロダクトから 4 つのソリューションのまとまりに進化。サプライチェーン計画の Decisions、採用の Talent（プレビュー）、顧客体験の Customer、医療向けの Health、という顔ぶれなの。

## 深く潜ってみよう

OpenAI 連携は 3 コンポーネントで、いまはどれも limited preview だよ。

- OpenAI モデル（GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.4）を Bedrock の API から呼べる
- Codex on Amazon Bedrock は AWS の認証情報で使えて、Codex CLI・デスクトップアプリ・Visual Studio Code 拡張から利用できる
- Bedrock Managed Agents powered by OpenAI は、OpenAI harness で動く本番向けのエージェント

先週のローンチは、週明けに手を動かせる系も多めなの。

- [EC2 M8in / M8ib](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/amazon-ec2-m8in-m8ib/) が GA。カスタム第 6 世代 Intel Xeon と第 6 世代 Nitro で、M6in / M6ib 比 最大 43 パーセントの性能アップ。M8in は 600 Gbps のネットワーク帯域だよ。
- メモリ最適化の [R8in / R8ib](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/amazon-ec2-r8in-r8ib/)（SAP HANA など向け）と、ネットワーク最適化の [C8ine / M8ine](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/ec2-c8ine-m8ine/)（vCPU あたり最大 2.5 倍のパケット性能）も GA になったよ。
- [Bedrock AgentCore](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/05/bedrock-agentcore-optimization-preview/) に、本番のトレースを分析して改善案を出す最適化機能（プレビュー）が追加。適用前には承認が必要なの。
- [AWS Lambda が Ruby 4.0](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/aws-lambda-adds-ruby/) に対応。中国リージョンや GovCloud (US) を含む全リージョンで使えるよ。

そのほか、[Amazon Q Developer の一部が end-of-support](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/devops/amazon-q-developer-end-of-support-announcement/) に。IDE プラグインと有償プランは 2027年4月30日で終了、新規登録は 2026年5月15日で停止で、最新モデルは Kiro に集約されていく流れなの。楽しい話題では [AWS 10,000 AIdeas コンペの受賞者発表](https://builder.aws.com/content/3D5gTWIjP2zvKncBZBCs849xRqn/aws-10000-aideas-competition-meet-the-winners)や、[AWS Student Builder Groups](https://builder.aws.com/content/3C075iQJeEx03mnzHwmXO9zdgEG/aws-student-builder-groups) の始動もあったよ。5 月は [AWS Summits](https://aws.amazon.com/events/summits/) や [AWS Community Days](https://aws.amazon.com/events/community-day/) も各地で開催されるの。

## まとめ

- What's Next with AWS 2026 で、Amazon Quick・Amazon Connect・OpenAI 連携の 3 本柱が発表
- OpenAI の GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.4、Codex、Managed Agents が Bedrock へ（すべて limited preview）
- EC2 M8 / R8 / C8 系が GA、Lambda は Ruby 4.0 対応で今すぐ触れる
- Amazon Q Developer は段階的に end-of-support、最新モデルは Kiro へ移行

Bedrock に OpenAI を足したい人、新しい EC2 で性能を伸ばしたい人、そして週明けに何か試したくてうずうずしているエンジニアみんなに刺さる回だよ！
title_en: What's Next with AWS 2026 Is Here! Amazon Quick, OpenAI on Bedrock, and New GA Instances
summary_en: A tour of the AWS Weekly Roundup (May 4, 2026): the What's Next with AWS 2026 event unveiled Amazon Quick's expansion, Amazon Connect's four solutions, and OpenAI models on Bedrock, plus new GA EC2 instances and Lambda's Ruby 4.0 support.
body_md_en:
Hey everyone, it's me, Shiichan! This week's AWS news is absolutely packed, and just reading it gets me excited. A big event, new AI models, freshly GA instances — there's so much. Let me walk you through it!

## What was announced?

AWS published the "AWS Weekly Roundup (May 4, 2026)" on its News Blog. The star this time is the online event held on April 28, "What's Next with AWS 2026." AWS CEO Matt Garman and others took the stage alongside OpenAI leadership, and the theme was how we all use agents to transform the way we work.

Three headline announcements came out of it.

- [Amazon Quick](https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/aws/amazon-quick-desktop-ai-assistant) expands with a desktop app and new features
- [Amazon Connect](https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/aws/amazon-connect-ai-business-set) grows into four agentic AI solutions
- [AWS and OpenAI expand their partnership](https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/aws/bedrock-openai-models), bringing OpenAI models to Amazon Bedrock

If you want the full event recap, the [Top announcements of the What's Next with AWS, 2026](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/top-announcements-of-the-whats-next-with-aws-2026/) post lays it all out.

## Why it matters

Until now, Bedrock has been the place to call many models — starting with Anthropic's Claude — from a single API. Adding OpenAI's flagship models widens your options a lot. And because you can apply security, governance, and cost controls centrally on the Bedrock side, your operations don't fragment even as the number of models you use grows.

## What changes

Amazon Quick now has a desktop app (Preview) that can reach your local files, calendar, and communications without opening a browser. You can even sign up without an AWS account, using email or Google, Apple, GitHub, or Amazon credentials. It also added the ability to generate documents, presentations, infographics, and images straight from chat, plus expanded native integrations with Google Workspace, Zoom, Airtable, Dropbox, and Microsoft Teams.

Amazon Connect, meanwhile, grew from a single product into a set of four solutions: Decisions for supply chain planning, Talent for hiring (Preview), Customer for customer experiences, and Health for healthcare.

## Dive Deep

The OpenAI collaboration has three components, all currently in limited preview.

- OpenAI models (GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.4) callable through the Bedrock APIs
- Codex on Amazon Bedrock, which works with your AWS credentials and is available from the Codex CLI, desktop app, and Visual Studio Code extension
- Bedrock Managed Agents powered by OpenAI, production-ready agents built with the OpenAI harness

Last week's launches include plenty you can actually get your hands on.

- [EC2 M8in / M8ib](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/amazon-ec2-m8in-m8ib/) are GA. With custom 6th-gen Intel Xeon and 6th-gen Nitro, they deliver up to 43 percent higher performance versus M6in / M6ib, and M8in offers 600 Gbps of network bandwidth.
- Memory-optimized [R8in / R8ib](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/amazon-ec2-r8in-r8ib/) (for workloads like SAP HANA) and network-optimized [C8ine / M8ine](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/ec2-c8ine-m8ine/) (up to 2.5x higher packet performance per vCPU) are GA too.
- [Bedrock AgentCore](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/05/bedrock-agentcore-optimization-preview/) added an optimization capability (Preview) that analyzes production traces and proposes improved system prompts and tool descriptions, with your approval required before anything is applied.
- [AWS Lambda now supports Ruby 4.0](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/aws-lambda-adds-ruby/), available in all AWS Regions including the China Regions and GovCloud (US).

Beyond that, part of [Amazon Q Developer is reaching end-of-support](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/devops/amazon-q-developer-end-of-support-announcement/): IDE plugins and paid plans end on April 30, 2027, new signups stop on May 15, 2026, and the latest models are consolidating into Kiro. On the fun side, the [AWS 10,000 AIdeas Competition winners](https://builder.aws.com/content/3D5gTWIjP2zvKncBZBCs849xRqn/aws-10000-aideas-competition-meet-the-winners) were announced, and [AWS Student Builder Groups](https://builder.aws.com/content/3C075iQJeEx03mnzHwmXO9zdgEG/aws-student-builder-groups) launched. In May, [AWS Summits](https://aws.amazon.com/events/summits/) and [AWS Community Days](https://aws.amazon.com/events/community-day/) are happening in many cities.

## Wrap-up

- What's Next with AWS 2026 unveiled three pillars: Amazon Quick, Amazon Connect, and the OpenAI collaboration
- OpenAI's GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.4, Codex, and Managed Agents are coming to Bedrock (all in limited preview)
- EC2 M8 / R8 / C8 families are GA, and Lambda's Ruby 4.0 support is something you can try right now
- Amazon Q Developer is winding down toward end-of-support, with the latest models moving to Kiro

This roundup is for anyone who wants to add OpenAI to Bedrock, push performance with the new EC2 instances, and every engineer itching to try something new next week!
